_comment: County (and county-equivalent) reference data for QSO party analysis.
Structure: one entry per host state (or multi-state group), keyed by state abbreviation.
format    - how IN-STATE (host) stations encode their county in their TX exchange:
'3letter'  - 3-char abbrev (e.g. ALB, ERI) — used by NYQP and most single-state parties
'4letter'  - 4-char abbrev (e.g. ALBA, ERIE) — used by some parties
'SSCCC'    - 2-letter state prefix + county abbrev (e.g. VTWIN, MABER)
used by multi-state parties like NEQP where VT, NH, MA etc.
each send their own state+county combo
counties  - dict of exchange-code -> full county (or county-equivalent) name
Note: 'counties' is used loosely — some states have reorganized.
e.g. CT replaced counties with 'Planning Regions' / administrative
districts circa 2022-2023. Use whatever the contest exchange specifies.
Non-host stations always send their 2-letter state abbreviation regardless of format.
When adding a new state or party:
1. Add its entry here with the correct format and county list
2. Add a config/[party]_[year].json pointing to it via host_state
3. Add a GeoJSON boundaries file to reference/[state]_counties.json
4. The pipeline will pick up the county list automatically via host_state in the config
